title: TUI
summary: Covers Hermes's modern terminal UI (TUI) built on Node.js, with rich overlays, non-blocking input, LaTeX rendering, light-terminal detection, and live session status.

env_vars.name: HERMES_TUI
env_vars.purpose: Set to 1 to use TUI by default instead of classic CLI

env_vars.name: HERMES_TUI_RESUME
env_vars.purpose: Set to 1 or a session ID to auto-resume on launch

env_vars.name: HERMES_TUI_THEME
env_vars.purpose: Force light/dark theme or a raw 6-char background hex

env_vars.name: HERMES_TUI_DIR
env_vars.purpose: Path to a prebuilt TUI bundle directory

cli_commands.purpose: Resume a specific session by ID

config_keys.description: Global accordion default: hidden | collapsed | expanded
Override details mode for thinking section
Override details mode for tools section
Override details mode for activity section
Enable mouse tracking in the TUI
Busy indicator style: kaomoji | emoji | unicode | ascii

slash_commands.purpose: Modal model picker grouped by provider
Install VS Code / Cursor / Windsurf terminal bindings

code_examples.caption: Launch and resume the TUI
code_examples.code:
hermes --tui
hermes --tui -c
hermes --tui -r 20260409_000000_aa11bb
export HERMES_TUI=1
hermes

code_examples.caption: TUI-specific display configuration
code_examples.code:
display:
  details_mode: collapsed
  sections:
    thinking: expanded
    tools: expanded
    activity: collapsed
  mouse_tracking: true
  tui_status_indicator: kaomoji

gotchas: Node.js ≥ 20 is required — hermes doctor verifies this
On first launch, TUI deps install into ui-tui/node_modules (one-time, a few seconds)
The dashboard side-process is not supervised — if it crashes it stays down until container restart
If TUI fails to launch (no Node, missing bundle, TTY issue), Hermes falls back to classic CLI
Sessions are shared between TUI and classic CLI via state.db
full_text:
# TUI

# TUI

The TUI is the modern front-end for Hermes — a terminal UI backed by the same Python runtime as the [Classic CLI](cli.md). Same agent, same sessions, same slash commands; a cleaner, more responsive surface for interacting with them.

It's the recommended way to run Hermes interactively.

## Launch

```bash
# Launch the TUI
hermes --tui

# Resume the latest TUI session
hermes --tui -c
hermes --tui --continue

# Resume a specific session by ID or title
hermes --tui -r 20260409_000000_aa11bb
hermes --tui --resume "my t0p session"

# Run source directly (for TUI contributors)
hermes --tui --dev
```

You can also enable it via env var:

```bash
export HERMES_TUI=1
hermes          # now uses the TUI
```

## Why the TUI

- **Instant first frame** — the banner paints before the app finishes loading
- **Non-blocking input** — type and queue messages before the session is ready
- **Rich overlays** — model picker, session picker, approval prompts as modal panels
- **Live session panel** — tools and skills fill in progressively
- **Mouse-friendly selection** — drag to highlight with a uniform background
- **Alternate-screen rendering** — differential updates, no flicker

## Requirements

- **Node.js** ≥ 20 — the TUI runs as a subprocess launched from the Python CLI
- **TTY** — piping stdin falls back to single-query mode

On first launch Hermes installs the TUI's Node dependencies into `ui-tui/node_modules` (one-time, a few seconds).

### External prebuild

```bash
export HERMES_TUI_DIR=/path/to/prebuilt/ui-tui
hermes --tui
```

The directory must contain `dist/entry.js`.

## Keybindings

Keybindings match the Classic CLI exactly. Differences:

- **Mouse drag** highlights text with a uniform selection background
- **`Cmd+V` / `Ctrl+V`** first tries normal text paste, then falls back to image attach
- **`/terminal-setup`** installs local VS Code / Cursor / Windsurf terminal bindings
- **Slash autocompletion** opens as a floating panel with descriptions
- **`Ctrl+X`** — delete a queued message; **`Esc`** cancels editing

## Slash commands

TUI-owned commands produce richer output or render as overlays:

| Command | TUI behavior |
|---------|--------------|
| `/help` | Overlay with categorized commands, arrow-key navigable |
| `/sessions` | Modal session picker |
| `/model` | Modal model picker grouped by provider |
| `/skin` | Live preview — theme change applies as you browse |
| `/details` | Toggle verbose tool-call details |
| `/usage` | Rich token / cost / context panel |
| `/agents` (alias `/tasks`) | Observability overlay — live subagent tree |
| `/reload` | Re-reads `~/.hermes/.env` without restart |
| `/mouse` | Toggle mouse tracking on/off |

## LaTeX math rendering

The TUI renders LaTeX math inline: `$E = mc^2$` and `$$\frac{a}{b}$$` render as Unicode-formatted math. Always-on.

## Light-terminal detection

Detection works in three layers:
1. `HERMES_TUI_THEME` env var — highest priority. Values: `light`, `dark`, or raw 6-char hex
2. `COLORFGBG` env var
3. Terminal background probe via OSC 11

```bash
export HERMES_TUI_THEME=light
```

## Busy indicator styles

```yaml
display:
  tui_status_indicator: kaomoji   # kaomoji | emoji | unicode | ascii
```

Or in-session: `/indicator emoji`.

## Auto-resume

```bash
export HERMES_TUI_RESUME=1          # most-recent TUI session
export HERMES_TUI_RESUME=<session-id>   # specific session
```

## Status line

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `starting agent…` | Session ID is live; tools still coming online |
| `ready` | Agent is idle |
| `thinking…` / `running…` | Agent is reasoning or running a tool |
| `interrupted` | Current turn was cancelled |
| `forging session…` / `resuming…` | Initial connect or `--resume` handshake |

The status line also shows working directory with git branch and per-prompt elapsed time.

## Configuration

```yaml
display:
  skin: default
  personality: helpful
  details_mode: collapsed    # hidden | collapsed | expanded
  sections:
    thinking: expanded       # always open
    tools: expanded          # always open
    activity: collapsed
  mouse_tracking: true
```

Runtime toggles:
- `/details [hidden|collapsed|expanded|cycle]` — set global mode
- `/details <section> [hidden|collapsed|expanded|reset]` — override one section

## Sessions

Sessions are shared between the TUI and the classic CLI — both write to the same `~/.hermes/state.db`.

## Reverting to the classic CLI

Launching `hermes` (without `--tui`) stays on the classic CLI. If the TUI fails to launch, Hermes prints a diagnostic and falls back.

## See also

- [CLI Interface](cli.md) — full slash command and keybinding reference (shared)
- [Sessions](sessions.md) — resume, branch, and history
- [Skins & Themes](features/skins.md) — theme the banner, status bar, and overlays
